Physical copies for Nintendo Switch are down at $39.99 shipped on Amazon right from the usual $50, while digital editions for both Switch and Switch 2 are marked down to $34.99 on the eShop – the eShop deals are scheduled to end on 5/7/26 at 02:59 a.m. EDT by the way. This is up to 30% off and a great chance to land the vintage-style Square Enix RPG at a discount if you haven’t yet. Scope out more of this week’s best Switch game deals below.

items.title: Today only: Score a Samsung 32-inch QHD 180Hz Odyssey G5 gaming monitor for $220 (Save $110)
items.summary:
Today only, as part of its Deals of the Day offers, Best Buy has listed one of Samsung’s 32-inch Odyssey G5 gaming monitors for $219.99 shipped. This G51F model, which is exclusive to Best Buy, originally hit the scene for $350, but it has more recently been going for $330. Today’s offer shaves $110 off that price to mark the lowest we have tracked for this particular version. While we have seen other Odyssey G5 displays – including the G55C, which is a similarly-specced model at Amazon – go for less, the G51F featured at Best Buy’s Deals of the Day offer has never gone for less. Besides that, it’s a solid choice for a 180Hz QHD gaming display from one of the big name brands like Samsung we feature a lot around here, so it’s definitely worth a look today.

items.title: Save $150 today on a 1TB Xbox Series X with Forza Horizon 6 Limited Edition controller and game bundle (Pre-order deal)
items.summary:
Ahead of the upcoming release of Forza Horizon 6 later this month, Best Buy is offering a bundle that knocks $150 off a 1TB Xbox Series X, bundled with the new limited-edition Forza Horizon 6 controller and a physical disc version of the game. You can pre-order the full package for $659.97 shipped right now, which effectively gets you the console at $499.99 as part of the bundle, compared to the $650 it has been going for recently at Amazon. It’s a great deal to scoop up, especially with Xbox getting a lot of positive attention lately, thanks to recent changes and stronger community engagement under its new leadership. On top of that, you’ll be ready to play the latest Forza title right away with both the included physical copy and themed controller.

items.title: Prep for summer hiking + other adventures with Ninja’s FrostVault Go backpack cooler down at a $150 2026 low, more
items.summary:
Over at Amazon, you can score the Ninja FrostVault Go Backpack Cooler back at $149.95 shipped and have a handy way to keep food chilled but not wet while trekking through summer adventures, with this price also available direct from the brand’s website, too. Normally $200 at full price, we’ve only seen one previous fall in 2026 to this same rate, while it’s only been beaten out by a drop further to $142 back during October’s 2025 Prime Day event. You’re getting a 25% markdown here while the deal lasts, cutting $50 off the price tag for the second-lowest rate we have tracked. We also spotted the similar Ninja FrostVault wheeled coolers starting from $250 right now, down from $499.

more…

items.title: Dyson’s Airwrap i.d. multi-styler drops down to $499 2026 low – and it arrives ahead of Mother’s Day!
items.summary:
The official Dyson Amazon storefront is currently offering the popular Airwrap i.d. Multi-Styler for curly to coily hair down at $499 shipped, while the model for straight to wavy hair sits $1 higher on the same landing page – plus, ordering now ensures it’ll be delivered by Mother’s Day. Normally $649 without discounts, we’ve seen the price fall as low as $499 since late December, only beaten out by a Christmas deal to the $479 low. You’re getting another chance to pick one up with a 23% markdown off the going rate, saving you $150 at the second-lowest price we have tracked – just $20 above that Christmas low that hasn’t appeared since.

more…

items.summary:

This model carries a $699 MSRP, though we’ve only seen it climb as high as $600 since late October, with discounts having gone as low as $510 up until today. Now, you’re getting a larger-than-ever $101 markdown off the going rate ($200 off the MSRP) for a new all-time low price.

items.title: Add Jackery’s 7-pound 292Wh Explorer 300 power station to your summer adventure kits while down at $189 (Reg. $279)
items.summary:
Through its official Amazon storefront, Jackery is offering its compact Explorer 300 292Wh Portable Power Station down at $188.99 shipped, beating out the brand’s direct website pricing by $90 (where it is sold out). While this model carries a $279 MSRP direct from the brand, we’re seeing a fall here at Amazon from $287, which has gone as low as $189 since mid-January. While we have seen the price go a little lower in the past, you’re still looking at a solid 32% markdown here off the going rate, saving you $90 off the MSRP (and $98 from the previous Amazon high) and landing it down amongst its best prices – just $30 above the all-time low we last saw during Black Friday. You can learn more about it and its bundle options below, or browse Jackery’s ongoing Mother’s Day Sale lineup in our original coverage here for other models.
